headline: The GH Cup 2026
description: The Guelph-Humber Pre-Law Society proudly hosts The GH Cup, an annual moot court competition that challenges participants to develop and present compelling legal arguments before a panel of judges. Open to undergraduate students across Ontario.
about:
The GH Cup is an annual inter-university moot court competition organized by the Guelph-Humber Pre-Law Society. Competitors present both written submissions (skeleton arguments) and oral advocacy before a panel of judges drawn from the legal profession.

The competition provides a valuable opportunity for undergraduate students to develop the skills they'll need in law school and beyond, while networking with peers and legal professionals from across the province.
howItWorks: Teams of two compete on behalf of either the appellant or respondent
A case problem is released in advance for preparation
Competitors submit written skeleton arguments before the oral rounds
Oral rounds are judged by volunteer lawyers, law students, and professors
Awards are given for best oral advocates and best written submissions
